channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jayden Daniels dazzles again as Commanders clinch a playoff spot by beating Falcons 30-24 in OT
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — Jayden Daniels felt tired. Tired from a game that went well past 60 minutes. Tired from running all over the field for the better part of three-plus hours. It was all worth it after carrying the Washington Commanders into the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Saquon Barkley tops 2,000 yards rushing and moves within 100 of Dickerson's record
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley normally would have tossed his game-used cleats to the kid who asked for the pair as the star Philadelphia Eagles running back ran off the field and back to the locker room. Sorry, kid. Not today.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFC's No. 1 seed comes down to Vikings-Lions showdown at Detroit in Week 18
item.description: The NFC’s No. 1 seed will come down to the final game of the regular season when the Detroit Lions host the Minnesota Vikings next Sunday night. The winner takes the NFC North and gets a first-round playoff bye and home-field advantage until the Super Bowl. The loser becomes the No.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thompson-Herro fight leads to ejections of multiple players and coaches in Heat's victory in Houston
item.description: Rockets forward Amen Thompson threw Heat guard Tyler Herro to the floor to trigger an altercation that resulted in six ejections in the closing minute of Miami’s 104-100 victory over Houston on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Herro leads Heat over Rockets in game marred by fight and ejections in final minute
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Tyler Herro scored 27 points before being one of seven people ejected, six for their roles in a fight in the final minute of the Miami Heat's 104-100 victory over the Houston Rockets on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pat McAfee torches Colts following bad loss to Giants, elimination from playoff hunt
item.description: Former Indianapolis Colts punter Pat McAfee ripped his old team in a scathing post on social media after the team's loss to the New York Giants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moment of silence for former President Jimmy Carter held before the Falcons-Commanders game
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — Jimmy Carter was honored with a moment of silence before the Atlanta Falcons’ game at the Washington Commanders on Sunday night, hours after the 39th president of the United States died at the age of 100 in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hanna Cavinder praises boyfriend Carson Beck as Georgia star declares for NFL Draft
item.description: Hanna Cavinder, the Miami Hurricanes guard and social media sensation, congratulated her boyfriend, Georgia quarterback Carson Beck, on declaring for the 2025 NFL Draft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: Sam Darnold added another exploit to his career-altering season, passing for a personal-best 377 yards and three touchdowns as the Minnesota Vikings hung on to beat the Green Bay Packers 27-25 for their ninth consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: Dave Campbell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter, former US president, remembered in sports world after death
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter was remembered in the sports world on Sunday after news of his passing at the age of 100. The Braves and Falcons put out statements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saquon Barkley tops 2,000 yards rushing as Eagles beat Cowboys 41-7 to clinch NFC East
item.description: Barkley rushed for 167 yards to top 2,000 on the season, backup quarterback Kenny Pickett ran and threw for scores before departing with injured ribs, and the Eagles clinched the division title by routing the Dallas Cowboys 41-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Feels like 1979: Nottingham Forest moves into 2nd place behind rampant Liverpool in Premier League
item.description: The Premier League table is starting to have a 1979 kind of feel to it — with Liverpool at the top of the standings and Nottingham Forest in second place as the closest challenger.
item.dc:creator: MATTIAS KAREN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patriots fans chant 'Fire Mayo' as team suffers blowout loss to Chargers
item.description: New England Patriots fans yelled for the team to fire Jerod Mayo on Saturday as they watched them get blown out at home by the Los Angeles Chargers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz lose to Sixers for sixth straight home loss
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey each scored 32 points to lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 114-111 victory over the Utah Jazz on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ahkello Witherspoon makes last-minute interception to preserve Rams' 13-9 win over Arizona
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams spent the entire second half struggling on offense while bending just enough on defense to preserve a tiny lead. Arizona even backed Los Angeles into its own end zone in the final minute, needing just one big play to steal a win.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Point and Kucherov lead surging Lightning to 6-2 win over reeling Rangers
item.description: Brayden Point and Nikita Kucherov each had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning past the reeling New York Rangers 6-2 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Burrow throws 3-yard TD pass to Higgins in OT as the Bengals beat the Broncos 30-24
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — This time, near the very end of a mostly agonizing season, the Cincinnati Bengals made enough plays to win. A familiar connection closed it out.
item.dc:creator: MITCH STACY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mavs star Luka Doncic is latest pro athlete whose home was burglarized, business manager says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Luka Doncic of the Dallas Mavericks is the latest professional athlete whose home has been burglarized. The star guard's business manager told multiple media outlets Saturday there was a break-in at Doncic's home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago brings losing streak into matchup with Dallas
item.description: Dallas Stars (20-13-1, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (12-22-2, in the Central Division) Chicago; Sunday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks look to break their three-game skid when they play the Dallas Stars.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Shaq Barrett expected to re-sign with Bucs after being waived by Dolphins, reports say
item.description: Former Tampa Bay Buccaneers linebacker Shaq Barrett is expected to once again sign with the Bucs after he was waived by the Miami Dolphins, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tuch scores hat trick, Sabres beat Blackhawks 6-2 for 2nd straight win after 13-game skid
item.description: Alex Tuch scored a hat trick for the Buffalo Sabres in a 6-2 win against the Chicago Blackhawks on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls take on the Bucks on 3-game skid
item.description: Milwaukee Bucks (16-13, fifth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (13-18, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: Chicago enters the matchup against Milwaukee after losing three straight games. The Bulls are 2-5 against division opponents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls blow 21 point lead in 141-133 loss to Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jalen Johnson had a career-high 30 points and the Atlanta Hawks rallied from a late 21-point deficit by scoring 50 points in the fourth quarter in a 141-133 win over the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Brock Bowers closing in on record held by Mike Ditka
item.description: Las Vegas Raiders tight end Brock Bowers is poised to break Mike Ditka's rookie tight end record of 1,076 yards receiving in Week 17, with only ten yards needed to do so.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Beyoncé's performance highlighted Netflix's NFL debut on Christmas Day
item.description: Beyoncé provided more excitement than either game during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. Riding into her halftime appearance on a white horse, the 32-time Grammy winner rocked her hometown Houston crowd with a nearly 13-minute performance on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Reports: Pete Carroll interested in Bears' head-coaching job
item.description: Former Seattle Seahawks head coach Pete Carroll has expressed interest in becoming the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, who are looking to fill their head coaching vacancy for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: USF beats SJSU 41-39 in the Hawaii Bowl when Mac Harris knocks down a pass in the 5th overtime
item.description: Bryce Archie found Keshaun Singleton over the middle to give South Florida the lead in the fifth overtime and Mac Harris batted down a pass in the end zone to give the Bulls a 41-39 victory over San Jose State on Tuesday night in the Hawaii Bowl.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Breaking: Dave Fleming leaves CMU after 20 years
item.description: "I thank Coach Fleming for all his dedication and hard work at CMU," Colorado Mesa Athletic Director Joan McDermott said in a press release. "He is not only a great coach, but a special person and will be greatly missed.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KREX 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani wins 3rd AP Male Athlete of the Year award, tying Michael Jordan for 1 shy of record
item.description: The Japanese superstar caps 2024 by winning The Associated Press Male Athlete of the Year for the third time, tying him with basketball great Michael Jordan. He trails only four-time winners Lance Armstrong, Tiger Woods and LeBron James.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Eloy Jiménez, former White Sox slugger and top prospect, signs minor-league deal with Rays
item.description: Eloy Jiménez was once one of the top prospects in all of baseball coming up through the White Sox system, regarded as a foundational piece for a core of would-be White Sox future stars. On Monday, he signed a minor-league contract with the Tampa Bay Rays, according to MLB.com.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
